findings.evidence: `rg` shows the duplicated blocks: `Complex.lean:62` and `:96` are both `Finsupp.domLCongr (Function.Involutive.toPerm _ ...)`; lines `66-88` and `100-122` repeat the same single/apply/symm proof pattern with only `transpose` vs `rotate` changed.
findings.fix: Add a general `GridChain.relabelEquiv (R) (e : GridState n ≃ GridState n) : GridChain R n ≃ₗ[R] GridChain R n` with lemmas `relabelEquiv_single`, `relabelEquiv_apply` using `e.symm`, and `relabelEquiv_symm`; redefine `transposeEquiv` and `rotateEquiv` from it and keep the existing specialized simp lemmas as corollaries.
findings.issue: `transposeEquiv` and `rotateEquiv` still duplicate the same `Finsupp.domLCongr` wrapper and the same `_single`, `_apply`, and `_symm` proofs, specialized only to two involutions.

summary: The PR leaves the grid-chain relabeling API at two duplicated special cases instead of the natural `GridState n ≃ GridState n` level. Factor the common `Finsupp.domLCongr` construction and lemmas once, then derive transpose and rotate as special cases.
